text: "This present city, Münster, is indeed called the New Jerusalem. But the New Jerusalem can have only one king, and that is Christ; and all the saints shall serve Him in it, as now we are beginning to do here. Only those who stand with us shall enter it."

prompt: On the holding of goods in common
text: "All possessions are to be held in common. It is written in Acts: 'All who believed had everything in common.' It is unjust that one man should have more than another, especially now, when the poor are oppressed and the rich walk over the needy. So we have laid aside our private possessions, that the one may use what the other lacks."

"What the unrighteous power carries out in the great houses of banking is contrary to Christian teaching. To lend money at interest is usury, and God has forbidden it twice in the Scripture. I hate that man who makes usury of my people, says the Lord of hosts."
